title: cyperus oblongus subsp. flavus
intro: Cyperus oblongus subsp. flavus, also known as yellow papyrus, is a subspecies of the Cyperus oblongus plant, a type of sedge that is native to tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cyperus oblongus subsp. flavus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support digestive health, making it a valuable herbal remedy. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
